zh.homeIntro1: 以赛亚（Isaiah）是旧约圣经中最重要的先知之一，活跃于犹大王国乌西雅王至希西家王在位期间（约公元前740-700年）。以赛亚书是旧约先知书中篇幅最长的一卷，共66章，涵盖审判、救赎与末世异象。
zh.homeIntro2: 以赛亚的预言以弥赛亚预言最为著名，包括「以马内利」的预言（7:14）、「奇妙策士、全能的神」（9:6）、「耶西的根」（11:1）以及「受苦仆人」诗篇（52-53章）。这些预言在基督教传统中被视为对耶稣基督的预指。
zh.homeIntro3: 除弥赛亚预言外，以赛亚还预言了巴比伦的覆灭、亚述的入侵、列国的审判以及「新天新地」的末世异象。学者通常将以赛亚书分为「第一以赛亚」（1-39章）和「第二以赛亚」（40-66章）两部分。本站整理了以赛亚书中的重要预言，并附历史验证分析。
zh.coreQuote: 「因有一婴孩为我们而生，有一子赐给我们，政权必担在他的肩头上。他名称为奇妙策士、全能的神、永在的父、和平的君。」——以赛亚书 9:6
keyPointsList: 约前740年 —— 以赛亚蒙召，在圣殿中见异象（第6章）
约前735年 —— 以马内利预言，亚兰-以法莲危机期间（第7章）
前722年 —— 北国以色列被亚述灭亡，应验审判预言
前701年 —— 亚述围困耶路撒冷，希西家祈祷获救（第36-37章）
前539年 —— 巴比伦被波斯攻陷，应验以赛亚对巴比伦的审判预言
sourceList: 预言原文依据和合本及 NRSV 英译本，参考死海古卷以赛亚书全卷（Dead Sea Scrolls Digital Library）
历史验证基于考古学发现和古代近东文献（Wikipedia: Book of Isaiah）
学术分析参考《以赛亚书》注释文献，编者观点不代表学术定论
站点图标采用燃烧的火炭符号——取自以赛亚蒙召异象，撒拉弗以火炭洁净先知之口（以赛亚书6:6-7）

en.homeIntro1: Isaiah is one of the most important prophets in the Hebrew Bible, active during the reigns of Kings Uzziah through Hezekiah of Judah (~740-700 BC). The Book of Isaiah is the longest prophetic book in the Old Testament, spanning 66 chapters of judgment, redemption, and eschatological visions.
en.homeIntro2: Isaiah is best known for his messianic prophecies, including the Immanuel prophecy (7:14), 'Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God' (9:6), the Branch of Jesse (11:1), and the Suffering Servant poems (chapters 52-53). In Christian tradition, these are understood as pointing to Jesus Christ.
en.homeIntro3: Beyond messianic prophecies, Isaiah foretold the fall of Babylon, the Assyrian invasion, judgment upon nations, and the eschatological vision of 'new heavens and a new earth.' Scholars typically divide the book into First Isaiah (chapters 1-39) and Deutero-Isaiah (chapters 40-66). This site compiles key prophecies from the Book of Isaiah with historical verification.
en.coreQuote: "For to us a child is born, to us a son is given, and the government will be on his shoulders. And he will be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace." — Isaiah 9:6
keyPointsList: ~735 BC —— Immanuel prophecy during Syro-Ephraimite Crisis (Chapter 7)
722 BC —— Northern Kingdom of Israel falls to Assyria, fulfilling judgment prophecy
701 BC —— Assyria besieges Jerusalem, Hezekiah's prayer answered (Chapters 36-37)
539 BC —— Babylon falls to Persia, fulfilling Isaiah's judgment on Babylon
sourceList: Prophecy texts based on CUV and NRSV translations, referencing the Great Isaiah Scroll from the Dead Sea Scrolls (Dead Sea Scrolls Digital Library)
Historical verification based on archaeological findings and ancient Near Eastern records (Wikipedia: Book of Isaiah)
Scholarly analysis references Isaiah commentary literature; editorial opinions do not represent academic consensus
Site icon: burning coal symbol — from Isaiah's calling vision, where a seraph purified the prophet's lips with a burning coal (Isaiah 6:6-7)
